citations.description: File references ticket ID in content but this is not the commit trailer
CI enforces rebar-ticket: trailer, but enforcement does not substitute for direct verification of the commit message
Ticket comment says 'commit 143d5074e' but a ticket comment attestation cannot satisfy a codebase-verifiable criterion per verification rules

findings.criterion: The report is committed on main with a rebar-ticket trailer referencing this ticket.
findings.detail: The file docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md exists in the repository and is readable, confirming it has been committed. However, the specific AC2 verification requirement — that the commit message includes a 'rebar-ticket: d39b' trailer — requires running `git log -1 --format=%B -- docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md | grep -qi 'rebar-ticket: d39b'`, which is git metadata not accessible via file-read tools. The file itself references the ticket ID (line 3: 'Ticket: d39b-8c45-385c-418a'), and the repo enforces the commit-trailer convention via CI (docs/commit-ticket-trailer.md), but I cannot verify the actual commit message content. Per the bounded-search rule, the git trailer is not demonstrably confirmed.
findings.remediation: Run `git log -1 --format=%B -- docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md` and confirm the output contains 'rebar-ticket: d39b'. If the trailer is missing, amend the commit to add it and force-push (or make a fixup commit).

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
